lines.target: We will now look at the future tense.
lines.base: Nos referiremos ahora al tiempo [de] futuro.
lines.pronunciation: .. fiuché tens. — 4 ... ulil dress, luél dress ; dzeil dress. — 9 ... meinli... sádchesstshéns... — 12 ... tiubs on straik aél.. — 13 ... foun... ¡ul bit feit ?

lines.target: You form the future by putting “will” in front of nearly all our verbs.
lines.base: Se forma el futuro poniendo 'will' ante casi todos los verbos.

lines.target: For instance, the verb “to dress”, in the future, becomes “I will dress; you will dress”.
lines.base: Por ejemplo: vestirse -> yo me vestiré.

lines.target: We continue with the contraction: “we'll dress, you'll dress, they'll dress”.
Let's look at some sentences in the future:
I'll go to the cinema tonight if you'll come with me.
lines.base: Iré al cine esta noche si usted viene conmigo.

lines.target: You will learn English quickly if you read a lesson every day.
lines.base: Aprenderá inglés rápidamente si lee una lección cada día.

lines.target: The word “shall” is mainly used for suggestions: It's raining, shall we take a taxi?
lines.base: 'Shall' se utiliza para sugerencias: ¿cogemos un taxi?

lines.target: How much money will you need? Shall I give you some more?
lines.base: ¿Cuánto dinero necesitarás? ¿Quieres que te dé algo más?

lines.target: Will you give me some more coffee, please?
lines.base: ¿Quiere darme algo más [de] café, por favor?

lines.target: How will you go to work? The tube's on strike. — I'll take my car.
lines.base: ¿Cómo irá al trabajo? El metro está en huelga. — Cogeré mi coche.

lines.target: Shall I phone the office and tell them you'll be late?
lines.base: ¿Quiere que llame a la oficina y les diga que llegará tarde?

lines.target: I'll drive; you'll be late; you will have problems.
lines.base: Conduciré yo; llegarás tarde; tendrá problemas.

gap_candidates.target: Let's look at some sentences in the future:
How much money will you need? Shall I give you some more? (5) (6)
Will you give me some more coffee, please?
How will you go to work? The tube's on strike. — I'll take my car.
Shall I phone the office and tell them you'll be late?
I'll drive; you'll be late; you will have problems.
